title: setaria glauca var. corrugata
intro: Setaria glauca var. corrugata, also known as corrugated foxtail, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family, native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Setaria glauca var. corrugata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using its leaves in a topical poultice to apply directly to affected areas.
